functional_purpose: Receives DICOM studies, derives patient-identifier and source-platform metadata, and submits them to OpenEyes PayloadProcessor. Eligible Zeiss IOLMaster objects are also written to the IOLMasterImport incoming folder.
business_process: Ingesting ophthalmic imaging and routing IOLMaster biometry files for import.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after the source transformer and waits for processing.
main_filters: No connector filter; source transformer removes the IOLMaster file destination unless manufacturer is Zeiss, model contains iolmaster, and the SOP Class UID is supported.
main_transformer_stages: Map DICOM patient, study, device, and AE-title tags.
Derive identifier_type and institution_id from the local AE title, defaulting from configurationMap:defaultPatientIdentifierType.
Set source_platform from AE titles and conditionally remove the IOLMaster file writer.
error_handling: No explicit error transformer or error script is configured.
retry_behaviour: Both destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0 (retry interval configured as 10000 ms).
queue_behaviour: Destination queues are disabled; the source connector has a 1000-message buffer.
logging: No explicit logger calls; production message content and DICOM attachments are retained, creating a PII retention risk.

evidence.claim: Wales DICOM receives DICOM on port 11118.
evidence.location: Wales/DICOM.xml: sourceConnector DICOM Listener (lines 7-60, 280)

evidence.claim: It posts application/dicom with request_type=dicom_request to PayloadProcessor and uses preemptive Basic authentication with a redacted password.
evidence.location: Wales/DICOM.xml: destination 'PayloadProcessor API Send' (lines 355-470)

evidence.claim: The source transformer reads configurationMap:defaultPatientIdentifierType and conditionally retains the IOLMaster file writer only for supported Zeiss IOLMaster DICOM objects.
evidence.location: Wales/DICOM.xml: source transformer steps 'Extract identifier_type and institution_id from Local AET' and 'Remove the IOLMaster Import destination...' (lines 204-279)

functional_purpose: Accepts an HTTP demographic query, builds an HL7 PDQ query using ABUHB identifier code 139, sends it to the Welsh MPI SOAP service, and returns a simplified OpenEyes XML patient list.
business_process: Patient demographic lookup before clinical record selection.
message_format: XML request / HL7v2 PDQ query / XML response
ack_behaviour: HTTP response is created from the transformed MPI response after processing.
main_filters: No source or SOAP destination filter is configured.
main_transformer_stages: Set HL7 message timestamp and query identifiers.
Populate QPD query criteria from hosnum, nhsnum, givenname, familyname, and dob HTTP parameters.
Wrap the HL7 query in SOAP, extract the SOAP response, and transform K21 data to OpenEyes XML.
error_handling: No explicit error script is configured.
retry_behaviour: SOAP destination queueing is disabled and retryCount is 0.
logging: No explicit logger calls; production message content is retained for 14 days.

evidence.claim: Wales OpenEyes Query - ABUHB listens by HTTP on port 6668 and populates QPD criteria from request parameters.
evidence.location: Wales/OpenEyes Query - ABUHB.xml: sourceConnector and transformer steps 0-6 (lines 7-184)

evidence.claim: It sends InvokePatientDemographicsQuery to the Welsh MPI Web Service and transforms the SOAP/K21 response to OpenEyes XML.
evidence.location: Wales/OpenEyes Query - ABUHB.xml: destination 'Send Q21' and response destinations (lines 192-502, 717-890)

evidence.claim: ABUHB's concrete per-board parameter is QPD.3.2 identifier code 139, also used as vSender in the OpenEyes XML transform.
evidence.location: Wales/OpenEyes Query - ABUHB.xml: transformer 'Set hosnum or nhsnum parameter' and destination 'Create Response' XSLT (lines 71-97, 745-770)

functional_purpose: Receives PAS ADT messages over MLLP and updates OpenEyes patients and appointments through PASAPI V1. It separately handles patient merges and appointment cancellation/deletion events.
business_process: Synchronising patient demographics, encounters, appointments, and merges from the PAS into OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: The TCP HL7 response generator is configured for AA success, AE error, and AR rejection acknowledgements.
main_filters: PASAPI - Secondary Patient and PASAPI - Patient are split by A40 versus non-M05 events.
Clinic List - PUT accepts A01, A05, A08, A11, A03, A13, A02, and A12.
main_transformer_stages: Extract hospital/NHS identifiers, visit details, clinic and doctor codes, appointment status, and merge identifiers from HL7.
Transform patient data to PASAPI XML and PUT patient records.
PUT or DELETE PASAPI appointments according to ADT event filters, and PUT patient merges.
error_handling: Patient response transformers strip server log text, inspect warnings, and set responseErrorMessage; mapping scripts log extraction errors.
retry_behaviour: All HTTP destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0 (retry interval configured as 10000 ms).
queue_behaviour: Destination queues are disabled; no source queue is configured.
logging: Mapping scripts log errors and one DoctorCode script logs PD1.4.1, creating a PII-in-log risk.

evidence.claim: Wales PAS IN receives HL7V2 through a TCP Listener on port 6661 and produces AA, AE, and AR ACK codes.
evidence.location: Wales/PAS IN.xml: sourceConnector (lines 7-142)

evidence.claim: It uses preemptive Basic authentication with a redacted password to PUT PASAPI V1 Patient and PatientAppointment resources and DELETE appointments.
evidence.location: Wales/PAS IN.xml: destinations 'PASAPI - Patient' and 'Clinic List - PUT/DELETE' (lines 628-1103, 1107-1633)

evidence.claim: The appointment filter accepts A01, A05, A08, A11, A03, A13, A02, and A12; A38 drives appointment deletion and A40 drives PatientMerge.
evidence.location: Wales/PAS IN.xml: 'Appointment Filter', 'Clinic List - DELETE' filter, and 'PASAPI - Patient Merge' filter (lines 1360-1388, 1613-1629, 1860-1874)
